zh.result: 在ImgEdit Basic-Edit Suite上，PhysEdit相比强推理基线实现1.18倍加速（每样本64.3秒 vs 76.1秒），指令遵循度提升0.7%（CLIP-T 0.2283 vs 0.2266），身份保持基本持平（CLIP-I 0.8246 vs 0.8280），外观级编辑加速达1.52倍。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户更高效地执行物理一致的图像编辑，特别是需要不同推理深度的复杂编辑任务。

idea_spark.idea: 将CARD和SRM扩展到视频编辑，实现帧间自适应推理以保持时间一致性。
idea_spark.risk: 自适应推理可能引入额外计算开销，需平衡加速与质量；空间掩码可能过度限制推理区域导致遗漏全局上下文。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从指令异构性出发，将固定推理调度转化为条件计算问题，启发其他领域（如自然语言处理）的自适应推理设计。

en.tldr: PhysEdit achieves physically-consistent region-aware image editing via adaptive spatio-temporal reasoning, accelerating inference while maintaining quality.
en.motivation: Existing reasoning-based editors apply a fixed inference recipe to all instructions, ignoring heterogeneous demands in spatial coverage and reasoning depth.
en.method: Two inference-time modules without retraining the backbone: Complexity-Adaptive Reasoning Depth (CARD) predicts edit complexity and allocates reasoning steps and token length per sample; Spatial Reasoning Mask (SRM) extracts instruction-conditioned spatial priors from cross-attention to confine reasoning to relevant regions.
en.result: On ImgEdit Basic-Edit Suite, PhysEdit achieves 1.18x speedup (64.3s vs 76.1s per sample) over a strong baseline, with 0.7% improvement in instruction adherence (CLIP-T 0.2283 vs 0.2266) and comparable identity preservation (CLIP-I 0.8246 vs 0.8280); speedup reaches 1.52x on appearance-level edits.
en.help_to_user: Enables efficient physically-consistent image editing, especially for tasks requiring varying reasoning depth.

idea_spark.idea: Extend CARD and SRM to video editing for inter-frame adaptive reasoning to maintain temporal consistency.
idea_spark.risk: Adaptive reasoning may introduce overhead; spatial masking might over-constrain reasoning regions, missing global context.
idea_spark.inspiration: Transforming fixed inference schedules into conditional computation based on instruction heterogeneity inspires adaptive reasoning designs in other domains like NLP.

papers.title: Lottery BP: Unlocking Quantum Error Decoding at Scale
papers.summary: To enable fault tolerance on millions of qubits in real time, scalable decoding is necessary, which motivates this paper. Existing decoding algorithms (decoders), such as clustering, matching, belief propagation (BP), and neural networks, suffer from one or more of inaccuracy, costliness, and incompatibility, upon a broad set of quantum error correction codes, such as surface code, toric code, and bivariate bicycle code. Therefore, there exists a gap between existing decoders and an ideal decoder that is accurate, fast, general, and scalable simultaneously. This paper contributes in three aspects, including decoder, decoder architecture, and decoding simulator. First, we propose Lottery BP, a decoder that introduces randomness during decoding. Lottery BP improves the decoding accuracy over BP by 2~8 orders of magnitude for topological codes. To efficiently decode multi-round measurement errors, we propose syndrome vote as a pre-processing step before Lottery BP, which compresses multiple rounds of syndromes into one. Syndrome voting increases the latency margin of decoding and mitigates the backlog problem. Second, we design a PolyQec architecture that implements Lottery BP as a local decoder and ordered statistics decoding (OSD) as a global decoder, and it is configurable for surface/toric code and X/Z check. Since Lottery BP boosts the local decoding accuracy, PolyQec invokes the costly global OSD decoder less frequently over BP+OSD to enhance the scalability, e.g., 3~5 orders of magnitude less for topological codes. Third, to evaluate decoders fairly, we develop a PyTorch-based decoding simulator, Syndrilla, that modularizes the simulation pipeline and allows to extend new decoders flexibly. We formulate multiple metrics to quantify the performance of decoders and integrate them in Syndrilla. Running on GPUs, Syndrilla is 1~2 orders of magnitude faster than CPUs.

ai.tldr: 提出Lottery BP解码器、PolyQec架构和Syndrilla模拟器，大幅提升量子纠错解码的准确性和可扩展性。
ai.motivation: 现有解码器在准确性、速度和通用性上无法同时满足大规模量子纠错需求。
ai.method: 在BP解码中引入随机性（Lottery BP），并用综合征投票压缩多轮测量误差；设计PolyQec架构结合局部Lottery BP和全局OSD解码；开发模块化模拟器Syndrilla。
ai.result: Lottery BP将拓扑码解码精度提升2~8个数量级，PolyQec将全局OSD调用频率降低3~5个数量级，Syndrilla在GPU上比CPU快1~2个数量级。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何通过随机化和架构设计实现高效可扩展的量子解码。

idea_spark.idea: 将Lottery BP的随机化思想用于经典纠错码或神经网络解码中的不确定性处理。
idea_spark.risk: 随机化可能引入不可预测的延迟或解码失败概率，需在实时系统中谨慎评估。
idea_spark.inspiration: 综合征投票压缩多轮数据的方法可启发其他时序信号处理任务中的降噪或特征提取。

zh.tldr: 提出Lottery BP解码器、PolyQec架构和Syndrilla模拟器，大幅提升量子纠错解码的准确性和可扩展性。
zh.motivation: 现有解码器在准确性、速度和通用性上无法同时满足大规模量子纠错需求。
zh.method: 在BP解码中引入随机性（Lottery BP），并用综合征投票压缩多轮测量误差；设计PolyQec架构结合局部Lottery BP和全局OSD解码；开发模块化模拟器Syndrilla。
zh.result: Lottery BP将拓扑码解码精度提升2~8个数量级，PolyQec将全局OSD调用频率降低3~5个数量级，Syndrilla在GPU上比CPU快1~2个数量级。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何通过随机化和架构设计实现高效可扩展的量子解码。

idea_spark.idea: 将Lottery BP的随机化思想用于经典纠错码或神经网络解码中的不确定性处理。
idea_spark.risk: 随机化可能引入不可预测的延迟或解码失败概率，需在实时系统中谨慎评估。
idea_spark.inspiration: 综合征投票压缩多轮数据的方法可启发其他时序信号处理任务中的降噪或特征提取。

en.tldr: Proposes Lottery BP decoder, PolyQec architecture, and Syndrilla simulator to significantly improve accuracy and scalability of quantum error decoding.
en.motivation: Existing decoders cannot simultaneously achieve accuracy, speed, generality, and scalability for large-scale quantum error correction.
en.method: Introduces randomness into BP decoding (Lottery BP) and uses syndrome vote to compress multi-round measurement errors; designs PolyQec architecture combining local Lottery BP and global OSD; develops modular simulator Syndrilla.
en.result: Lottery BP improves decoding accuracy by 2~8 orders of magnitude for topological codes; PolyQec reduces global OSD invocations by 3~5 orders of magnitude; Syndrilla runs 1~2 orders of magnitude faster on GPU than CPU.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how randomization and architecture design enable efficient and scalable quantum decoding.

idea_spark.idea: Apply Lottery BP's randomization idea to classical error-correcting codes or uncertainty handling in neural network decoding.
idea_spark.risk: Randomization may introduce unpredictable latency or decoding failure probability, requiring careful evaluation in real-time systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Syndrome vote compressing multi-round data can inspire denoising or feature extraction in other sequential signal processing tasks.

papers.title: Remote SAMsing: From Segment Anything to Segment Everything
papers.summary: SAM2 produces high-quality zero-shot segmentation on natural images, but applying it to large remote sensing scenes exposes two problems: (1) its mask generator faces an inherent quality-coverage trade-off: strict thresholds yield precise masks but leave most of the image unsegmented, while relaxed thresholds increase coverage at the cost of mask quality; and (2) large images must be tiled, fragmenting objects across tile boundaries. We propose Remote SAMsing, an open-source pipeline that solves both problems without modifying SAM2 or requiring training data. For coverage, a multi-pass algorithm runs SAM2 repeatedly on each tile, painting accepted masks black between passes to simplify the scene for the next iteration, and relaxing quality thresholds only when coverage gains stagnate, ensuring that the most precise masks are always captured first. For spatial consistency, contextual padding and a parameter-free best-match merge reconstruct objects fragmented across tile boundaries. Evaluated on seven scenes (5~cm to 4.78~m GSD), the pipeline raises coverage from 30--68\% (single-pass SAM2) to 91--98\%. Ablation experiments quantify the contribution of each component to coverage and detection quality. Per-class evaluation shows that SAM2 transfers well to discrete RS objects (buildings 95\%, cars 82--93\% Det@0.5) with segment boundaries 3--8$\times$ more precise than SLIC and Felzenszwalb baselines. Tile size functions as an implicit scale parameter: reducing it from $1{,}000$ to 250 raises Det@0.5 from 56\% to 85\%, outperforming SAM2's built-in multi-scale mechanism. The pipeline generalizes to MNF false-color imagery without retraining (99.5\% ASA) and scales to production-sized images: a 1.94 billion pixel Potsdam mosaic achieved 97\% coverage without quality degradation.

ai.tldr: 提出Remote SAMsing流水线，在不修改SAM2或需要训练数据的情况下，解决遥感大图分割中的质量-覆盖权衡和跨瓦片对象碎片问题。
ai.motivation: SAM2在自然图像上表现优异，但直接应用于遥感大图时，其掩码生成器存在质量-覆盖权衡，且大图分块导致对象被截断。
ai.method: 多轮迭代算法：每轮运行SAM2，将已接受掩码涂黑简化场景，仅在覆盖增长停滞时放松质量阈值；采用上下文填充和无参数最佳匹配合并修复跨瓦片碎片。
ai.result: 在7个场景（5 cm至4.78 m GSD）上，覆盖从30-68%提升至91-98%；建筑物检测精度95%，汽车82-93%；瓦片大小作为隐式尺度参数，减小瓦片尺寸可提升检测精度。
ai.help_to_user: 提供即用型开源流水线，无需训练即可将SAM2适配到遥感大图分割，显著提升覆盖率和对象完整性。

idea_spark.idea: 将瓦片大小作为隐式尺度参数，通过调整瓦片尺寸控制分割粒度，可能替代传统多尺度机制。
idea_spark.risk: 多轮迭代可能增加计算开销，且对极端复杂场景的收敛性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用上下文填充和最佳匹配合并修复跨瓦片碎片，启发其他分块处理任务中的对象一致性恢复方法。

zh.motivation: SAM2在自然图像上表现优异，但直接应用于遥感大图时，其掩码生成器存在质量-覆盖权衡，且大图分块导致对象被截断。

idea_spark.risk: 多轮迭代可能增加计算开销，且对极端复杂场景的收敛性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用上下文填充和最佳匹配合并修复跨瓦片碎片，启发其他分块处理任务中的对象一致性恢复方法。

en.tldr: Proposes Remote SAMsing, an open-source pipeline that addresses the quality-coverage trade-off and tile-boundary fragmentation when applying SAM2 to large remote sensing images, without modifying SAM2 or requiring training data.
en.motivation: SAM2 excels on natural images but struggles with large remote sensing scenes due to inherent quality-coverage trade-off in its mask generator and object fragmentation caused by tiling.
en.method: A multi-pass algorithm runs SAM2 repeatedly on each tile, painting accepted masks black to simplify the scene, relaxing quality thresholds only when coverage gains stagnate; contextual padding and parameter-free best-match merge reconstruct objects across tile boundaries.
en.result: Coverage improved from 30-68% (single-pass SAM2) to 91-98% on seven scenes (5 cm to 4.78 m GSD); building detection 95%, cars 82-93% Det@0.5; tile size acts as an implicit scale parameter, reducing tile size from 1000 to 250 raises Det@0.5 from 56% to 85%.
en.help_to_user: Provides a ready-to-use open-source pipeline that adapts SAM2 to large-scale remote sensing segmentation without training, significantly improving coverage and object completeness.

idea_spark.idea: Using tile size as an implicit scale parameter to control segmentation granularity may replace traditional multi-scale mechanisms.
idea_spark.risk: Multi-pass iteration may increase computational cost, and convergence on extremely complex scenes needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Contextual padding and best-match merge for repairing cross-tile fragmentation inspire similar consistency restoration methods in other tiled processing tasks.

papers.title: DMDSC: A Dynamic-Margin Deep Simplex Classifier for Open-Set Recognition on Medical Image Datasets
papers.summary: Medical imaging datasets are often characterized by extreme class imbalances, where rare pathologies are significantly underrepresented compared to common conditions. This imbalance poses a dual challenge for Open-Set Recognition (OSR): models must maintain high classification accuracy on known classes while reliably rejecting unknown samples unseen during training in the clinical settings. While recently proposed Deep Simplex Classifier (DSC)~\cite{cevikalp2024reaching} and UnCertainty-aware Deep Simplex Classifier (UCDSC)~\cite{Aditya_2026_WACV} successfully leverage Neural Collapse to ensure maximal inter-class separation, they rely on a uniform margin that does not account for the varying densities of medical classes.   In this paper, we propose DMDSC an enhanced framework featuring a dynamic margin approach. Our approach automatically adapts class-specific margins based on label frequency, enforcing a higher penalty and tighter feature clustering for rare pathologies to counteract the effects of data imbalance. Extensive experiments conducted on diverse medical benchmarks on BloodMNIST\cite{medmnistv2}, OCTMNIST\cite{medmnistv2}, DermaMNIST\cite{medmnistv2}, and BreaKHis~\cite{spanhol2015dataset} datasets, demonstrate that our framework outperforms state-of-the-art methods.

ai.tldr: 提出DMDSC框架，通过动态边距方法解决医学图像开放集识别中的类别不平衡问题。
ai.motivation: 医学图像数据集常存在极端类别不平衡，罕见病理样本远少于常见病，而现有深度单纯形分类器使用统一边距，无法适应不同类别密度。
ai.method: 基于标签频率自动调整类别特定边距，对罕见病理施加更高惩罚和更紧的特征聚类。
ai.result: 在BloodMNIST、OCTMNIST、DermaMNIST和BreaKHis数据集上优于现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用动态边距提升开放集识别中罕见病理的检测能力。

idea_spark.idea: 结合标签频率与特征聚类紧密度，自适应调整分类边距。
idea_spark.risk: 边距调整可能对常见类别的分类精度产生轻微影响。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从类别频率与特征分布的关系出发，设计更鲁棒的开放集识别机制。

zh.tldr: 提出DMDSC框架，通过动态边距方法解决医学图像开放集识别中的类别不平衡问题。
zh.motivation: 医学图像数据集常存在极端类别不平衡，罕见病理样本远少于常见病，而现有深度单纯形分类器使用统一边距，无法适应不同类别密度。

zh.result: 在BloodMNIST、OCTMNIST、DermaMNIST和BreaKHis数据集上优于现有方法。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用动态边距提升开放集识别中罕见病理的检测能力。

idea_spark.risk: 边距调整可能对常见类别的分类精度产生轻微影响。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从类别频率与特征分布的关系出发，设计更鲁棒的开放集识别机制。

en.tldr: Proposes DMDSC, a dynamic-margin deep simplex classifier for open-set recognition on medical image datasets with class imbalance.
en.motivation: Medical datasets suffer from extreme class imbalance; existing deep simplex classifiers use a uniform margin that fails to account for varying class densities.
en.method: Automatically adapts class-specific margins based on label frequency, enforcing higher penalty and tighter feature clustering for rare pathologies.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art methods on BloodMNIST, OCTMNIST, DermaMNIST, and BreaKHis datasets.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how dynamic margins improve detection of rare pathologies in open-set recognition.

idea_spark.idea: Adapt classification margins based on label frequency and feature clustering tightness.
idea_spark.risk: Margin adjustment may slightly affect classification accuracy for common classes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Design more robust open-set recognition mechanisms by considering class frequency and feature distribution.

papers.title: A Model-based Visual Contact Localization and Force Sensing System for Compliant Robotic Grippers
papers.summary: Grasp force estimation can help prevent robots from damaging delicate objects during manipulation and improve learning-based robotic control. Integrating force sensing into deformable grippers negotiates trade-offs in cost, complexity, mechanical robustness, and performance. With the growing integration of RGB-D wrist cameras into robotic systems for control purposes, camera-based techniques are a promising solution for indirect visual force estimation. Current approaches mostly utilize end-to-end deep learning, which can be brittle when generalizing to new scenarios, while existing model-based approaches are unsuited to grasping and modern grasper geometries. To address these challenges, we developed a model-based visual force sensing approach integrating an iterative contact localization with generalization to unseen objects. The system extracts structural key points from wrist camera RGB-D images of deforming fin-ray-shaped soft grippers, and uses these key points to define parameters of an inverse finite element analysis simulation in Simulation Open Framework Architecture. The iterative contact localization sub-system utilizes a deep learning-based online 3D reconstruction and pose estimation pipeline to dynamically update contact location, and is robust to visual occlusion and unseen objects. Our system demonstrated an average root mean square error of 0.23 N and normalized root mean square deviation of 2.11% during the load phase, and 0.48 N and 4.34% over the entire grasping process when interacting with different objects under various conditions, showcasing its potential for real-time model-based indirect force sensing of soft grippers.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于模型的视觉力传感系统，通过腕部RGB-D相机和逆有限元分析，实现软体夹爪的接触定位与抓取力估计。

en.tldr: A model-based visual force sensing system using wrist RGB-D camera and inverse finite element analysis for contact localization and grasp force estimation on compliant grippers.
en.motivation: End-to-end deep learning methods lack generalization, and existing model-based approaches are unsuitable for grasping and modern gripper geometries.
en.method: Extracts structural keypoints from wrist camera RGB-D images to define parameters for inverse finite element simulation; iterative contact localization uses deep learning-based online 3D reconstruction and pose estimation to dynamically update contact location.
en.result: Average RMSE of 0.23 N and NRMSD of 2.11% during load phase; 0.48 N and 4.34% over entire grasp process across various objects and conditions.
en.help_to_user: Enables indirect force estimation for soft grippers via vision without additional hardware, improving manipulation safety and control precision.

idea_spark.idea: Combines inverse finite element analysis with online 3D reconstruction to dynamically update contact location, overcoming occlusion and generalizing to unseen objects.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality RGB-D images and real-time simulation, demanding significant computational resources.
idea_spark.inspiration: Integrating physics models with deep learning offers an interpretable force sensing solution for soft robotics.

papers.title: Beyond Decodability: Reconstructing Language Model Representations with an Encoding Probe
papers.summary: Probing is widely used to study which features can be decoded from language model representations. However, the common decoding probe approach has two limitations that we aim to solve with our new encoding probe approach: contributions of different features to model representations cannot be directly compared, and feature correlations can affect probing results. We present an Encoding Probe that reverses this direction and reconstructs internal representations of models using interpretable features. We evaluate this method on text and speech transformer models, using feature sets spanning acoustics, phonetics, syntax, lexicon, and speaker identity. Our results suggest that speaker-related effects vary strongly across different training objectives and datasets, while syntactic and lexical features contribute independently to reconstruction. These results show that the Encoding Probe provides a complementary perspective on interpreting model representations beyond decodability.

ai.tldr: 提出编码探针方法，从可解释特征重建语言模型内部表示，弥补解码探针的不足。
ai.motivation: 解码探针无法直接比较不同特征对表示的贡献，且特征相关性影响结果。
ai.method: 编码探针反向建模，用可解释特征重建模型内部表示。
ai.result: 说话人相关效应因训练目标和数据集而异，句法和词汇特征独立贡献于重建。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种新的模型表示解释视角，超越可解码性。

idea_spark.inspiration: 启发从表示重建角度理解模型内部机制。

papers.title: Unlearning What Matters: Token-Level Attribution for Precise Language Model Unlearning
papers.summary: Machine unlearning has emerged as a critical capability for addressing privacy, safety, and regulatory concerns in large language models (LLMs). Existing methods operate at the sequence level, applying uniform updates across all tokens despite only a subset encoding the knowledge targeted for removal. This introduces gradient noise, degrades utility, and leads to suboptimal forgetting. We propose TokenUnlearn, a token-level attribution framework that identifies and selectively targets critical tokens. Our approach combines knowledge-aware signals via masking, and entropy-aware signals to yield importance scores for precise token selection. We develop two complementary strategies: hard selection, applying unlearning only to high-importance tokens, and soft weighting, modulating gradient contributions based on importance scores. Both extend existing methods to token-level variants. Theoretical analysis shows token-level selection improves gradient signal-to-noise ratio. Experiments on TOFU and WMDP benchmarks across three model architectures demonstrate consistent improvements over sequence-level baselines in both forgetting effectiveness and utility preservation.

ai.tldr: 提出TokenUnlearn框架，通过令牌级归因精确识别关键令牌，实现更有效的语言模型遗忘。
ai.motivation: 现有序列级遗忘方法对所有令牌统一更新，引入梯度噪声，降低效用并导致次优遗忘。
ai.method: 结合知识感知掩码和熵感知信号计算重要性分数，设计硬选择和软加权两种策略，将现有方法扩展为令牌级变体。
ai.result: 在TOFU和WMDP基准上，三种模型架构均一致优于序列级基线，遗忘效果和效用保持均有提升。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户更精准地移除语言模型中的特定知识，同时保留模型整体性能。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将令牌级重要性分数用于模型微调中的选择性更新，以提升数据效率。
idea_spark.risk: 令牌级选择可能引入新的偏差，需谨慎评估对模型公平性的影响。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受梯度信噪比分析启发，利用令牌级归因优化梯度更新方向。

papers.title: "What Are You Really Trying to Do?": Co-Creating Life Goals from Everyday Computer Use
papers.summary: Recent advances in user modeling make it feasible to conduct open-ended inference over a person's everyday computer use. Despite longstanding visions of systems that deeply understand our actions and the purposes they serve in our lives, existing systems only capture what a person is doing in the moment -- not why they are doing it -- limiting these systems to surface-level support. We introduce striving co-creation, a process for inferring broader life goals from unstructured observations of computer use. Grounded in Activity Theory and Emmons' personal strivings framework, our system progressively constructs a hierarchical representation of a person's activities. Crucially, strivings are difficult to fully resolve from observation alone, as the same action can be driven by many different goals. Our system therefore supports an editing interface that gives people agency over how they are understood by the system, feeding their corrections back into subsequent rounds of striving induction. In a week-long field deployment (N=14), we find that our co-creation process produces strivings that are representative of participants' long-term goals and gives them greater agency than baseline methods.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种从日常计算机使用中推断人生目标的共创过程，结合活动理论与个人奋斗框架，并通过编辑界面赋予用户对系统理解的掌控权。
ai.motivation: 现有系统只能捕捉用户当前行为，无法理解行为背后的深层目的，限制了支持深度。
ai.method: 基于活动理论和Emmons的个人奋斗框架，构建活动的层次化表示，并设计编辑界面让用户反馈修正，迭代优化目标推断。
ai.result: 一周实地部署（N=14）表明，该过程产生的奋斗目标能代表参与者的长期目标，且比基线方法赋予用户更强的掌控感。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户更清晰地认识自己的长期目标，并让系统提供更有意义的支持。

idea_spark.idea: 结合用户反馈循环，使AI系统在理解用户意图时更具可解释性和可控性。
idea_spark.risk: 用户可能不愿或无法准确反馈，导致推断偏差；隐私问题需谨慎处理。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将心理学框架与交互设计结合，实现人机协作的目标理解，启发更智能的个性化助手。

papers.title: Asymptotic Replacement for Quantum Channel Products with Applications to Inhomogeneous Matrix Product States
papers.summary: We develop a product-level trace-Dobrushin theory for finite-dimensional quantum channel products and apply it to deterministic and stationary random inhomogeneous matrix product states in left-canonical CPTP gauge. For a product of channels, the centered trace-Dobrushin coefficient quantifies the residual dependence on the input state, and its decay is the criterion for trace-norm forgetting. In the deterministic setting, this decay is equivalent to asymptotic replacement by a moving replacement channel. For two-sided products, pullback forgetting produces a unique boundary state, which determines the canonical replacement family. For stationary random CPTP cocycles, submultiplicativity of the product coefficient yields a trace-Dobrushin Lyapunov exponent. We prove that the almost sure negativity of this exponent is equivalent to quenched trace-norm memory loss and gives exponential forward and pullback convergence to a unique dynamically stationary random replacement channel. When the \(\varrho\)-mixing profile of the channel environment tends to zero, we obtain annealed super-polynomial estimates, while independence gives annealed exponential estimates. Finally, we transfer these estimates to inhomogeneous matrix product states whose auxiliary transfer maps are CPTP. These channel estimates transfer to deterministic and stationary random inhomogeneous MPS, giving infinite-volume limits of trace-closed finite-volume states, quantitative boundary stability, and correlation bounds governed by the same auxiliary product coefficients.

ai.tldr: 开发了有限维量子信道乘积的迹-Dobrushin理论，并应用于左规范CPTP规范下的非齐次矩阵乘积态。
ai.motivation: 研究量子信道乘积的渐近替代性质，以理解非齐次矩阵乘积态的无限体积极限和边界稳定性。
ai.method: 引入中心迹-Dobrushin系数，证明其衰减等价于确定性情形下的渐近替代；对随机CPTP余环，定义迹-Dobrushin Lyapunov指数并证明其负性等价于淬火迹范数记忆丧失。
ai.result: 建立了信道乘积的渐近替代定理，给出了确定性及随机非齐次MPS的无限体积极限、边界稳定性和关联界。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种分析量子信道乘积渐近行为的统一框架，可用于研究非齐次张量网络态的收敛性和记忆效应。

idea_spark.idea: 利用随机信道环境的混合性质（如ρ-混合）获得退火超多项式估计，可能为量子信道容量或量子记忆的鲁棒性提供新见解。
idea_spark.risk: 理论依赖于有限维假设和CPTP条件，扩展到无限维或非CPTP情形可能面临技术困难。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将经典Dobrushin理论量子化并用于张量网络态，启发我们探索其他经典统计力学工具在量子多体系统中的应用。

en.tldr: Develops a product-level trace-Dobrushin theory for finite-dimensional quantum channel products and applies it to inhomogeneous matrix product states in left-canonical CPTP gauge.
en.motivation: To understand the asymptotic replacement property of quantum channel products and its implications for infinite-volume limits and boundary stability of inhomogeneous MPS.
en.method: Introduces the centered trace-Dobrushin coefficient; shows its decay is equivalent to asymptotic replacement by a moving replacement channel in the deterministic case. For random CPTP cocycles, defines a trace-Dobrushin Lyapunov exponent and proves its negativity is equivalent to quenched trace-norm memory loss.
en.result: Establishes asymptotic replacement theorems for channel products, yielding infinite-volume limits, boundary stability, and correlation bounds for both deterministic and stationary random inhomogeneous MPS.
en.help_to_user: Provides a unified framework to analyze asymptotic behavior of quantum channel products, useful for studying convergence and memory effects in inhomogeneous tensor network states.

idea_spark.idea: Exploiting mixing properties (e.g., ρ-mixing) of the channel environment to obtain annealed super-polynomial estimates may offer new insights into quantum channel capacities or robustness of quantum memory.
idea_spark.risk: The theory relies on finite-dimensional and CPTP assumptions; extension to infinite dimensions or non-CPTP maps may face technical challenges.
idea_spark.inspiration: Quantizing classical Dobrushin theory for tensor network states inspires exploring other classical statistical mechanics tools in quantum many-body systems.

papers.title: Introduction to Mathematical Programming with Equilibrium Constraints (MPECs) and Bilevel Optimization
papers.summary: Our aim is to explain mathematical programs with equilibrium constraints (MPECs), motivate them through applications, present the main equivalent formulations of equilibrium constraints, and summarize the basic existence theory for optimal solutions. The central message is that an MPEC is an optimization problem whose feasible set is partly defined by another optimization, variational inequality, complementarity system, or equilibrium model.

ai.tldr: 本文介绍带平衡约束的数学规划（MPEC）及其与双层优化的关系，阐述平衡约束的等价形式、应用动机和最优解存在性理论。
ai.motivation: MPEC 在工程、经济等领域广泛出现，但因其非凸、非光滑特性，理论分析困难，需要系统介绍其基本概念和理论。
ai.method: 通过应用实例引出 MPEC，给出平衡约束的多种等价表述（如优化、变分不等式、互补系统），并总结最优解存在性理论。
ai.result: MPEC 的可行集部分由另一个优化、变分不等式、互补系统或均衡模型定义，存在性理论为后续算法设计提供基础。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解 MPEC 和双层优化的基本概念、等价形式及存在性理论，为后续研究或应用奠定理论基础。

idea_spark.idea: 利用互补系统将双层优化转化为单层约束优化，简化求解。
idea_spark.risk: 等价转化可能引入非凸性或计算复杂性，需谨慎处理。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从应用实例出发，可启发设计针对特定 MPEC 的高效算法。

zh.tldr: 本文介绍带平衡约束的数学规划（MPEC）及其与双层优化的关系，阐述平衡约束的等价形式、应用动机和最优解存在性理论。
zh.motivation: MPEC 在工程、经济等领域广泛出现，但因其非凸、非光滑特性，理论分析困难，需要系统介绍其基本概念和理论。
zh.method: 通过应用实例引出 MPEC，给出平衡约束的多种等价表述（如优化、变分不等式、互补系统），并总结最优解存在性理论。
zh.result: MPEC 的可行集部分由另一个优化、变分不等式、互补系统或均衡模型定义，存在性理论为后续算法设计提供基础。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解 MPEC 和双层优化的基本概念、等价形式及存在性理论，为后续研究或应用奠定理论基础。

idea_spark.idea: 利用互补系统将双层优化转化为单层约束优化，简化求解。
idea_spark.risk: 等价转化可能引入非凸性或计算复杂性，需谨慎处理。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从应用实例出发，可启发设计针对特定 MPEC 的高效算法。

en.tldr: This paper introduces mathematical programs with equilibrium constraints (MPECs) and bilevel optimization, presenting equivalent formulations, applications, and existence theory for optimal solutions.
en.motivation: MPECs arise widely in engineering and economics but are challenging due to nonconvexity and nonsmoothness, necessitating a systematic introduction to their fundamentals.
en.method: Motivates MPECs via applications, presents equivalent formulations (optimization, variational inequality, complementarity), and summarizes existence theory.
en.result: An MPEC's feasible set is partly defined by another optimization, variational inequality, complementarity system, or equilibrium model; existence theory underpins algorithm design.
en.help_to_user: Helps users grasp basic concepts, equivalent forms, and existence theory of MPECs and bilevel optimization, laying groundwork for further research or applications.

idea_spark.idea: Transform bilevel optimization into single-level constrained optimization via complementarity systems to simplify solution.
idea_spark.risk: Equivalent transformations may introduce nonconvexity or computational complexity, requiring careful handling.
idea_spark.inspiration: Application examples can inspire efficient algorithms tailored to specific MPECs.

papers.title: Faithful Extreme Image Rescaling with Learnable Reversible Transformation and Semantic Priors
papers.summary: Most recent extreme rescaling methods struggle to preserve semantically consistent structures and produce realistic details, due to the severely ill-posed nature of low- to high-resolution mapping under scaling factors of $16\times$ or higher. To alleviate the above problems, we propose FaithEIR, a diffusion-based framework for extreme image rescaling. Inspired by singular value decomposition, we develop learnable reversible transformation that enables invertible downscaling and upscaling in the latent space. To compensate for information loss due to quantization, we propose an adaptive detail prior, a high-frequency dictionary that captures the empirical average of commonly occurring structures in the training data. Finally, we design a lightweight pixel semantic embedder to provide semantic conditioning for the pretrained diffusion model. We present extensive experimental results demonstrating that our FaithEIR consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving superior reconstruction fidelity and perceptual quality. Our code, model weights, and detailed results are released at https://github.com/cshw2021/FaithEIR.

ai.tldr: 提出FaithEIR框架，利用可学习可逆变换和语义先验，在极端缩放因子下实现高保真图像重建。
ai.motivation: 现有极端缩放方法在16倍以上缩放因子下难以保持语义一致结构和生成逼真细节。
ai.method: 基于奇异值分解开发可学习可逆变换，实现潜空间可逆下采样和上采样；设计自适应细节先验（高频字典）补偿量化信息损失；设计轻量像素语义嵌入器为预训练扩散模型提供语义条件。
ai.result: 在多个数据集上优于现有方法，重建保真度和感知质量均显著提升。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何结合可逆变换与扩散模型解决极端图像缩放中的信息丢失和语义保持问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将可逆变换与扩散模型结合，利用高频字典作为先验，为极端缩放提供可解释的信息补偿机制。
idea_spark.risk: 高频字典可能过度依赖训练数据分布，导致对未见结构的泛化能力受限；可逆变换的量化误差仍可能累积。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受奇异值分解启发，将图像分解为可逆成分与高频细节，为极端缩放提供新范式。

zh.tldr: 提出FaithEIR框架，利用可学习可逆变换和语义先验，在极端缩放因子下实现高保真图像重建。
zh.motivation: 现有极端缩放方法在16倍以上缩放因子下难以保持语义一致结构和生成逼真细节。

zh.result: 在多个数据集上优于现有方法，重建保真度和感知质量均显著提升。

idea_spark.idea: 将可逆变换与扩散模型结合，利用高频字典作为先验，为极端缩放提供可解释的信息补偿机制。
idea_spark.risk: 高频字典可能过度依赖训练数据分布，导致对未见结构的泛化能力受限；可逆变换的量化误差仍可能累积。

en.tldr: Proposes FaithEIR, a diffusion-based framework with learnable reversible transformation and semantic priors for faithful extreme image rescaling.
en.motivation: Existing extreme rescaling methods struggle to preserve semantically consistent structures and produce realistic details under scaling factors of 16× or higher.
en.method: Develops learnable reversible transformation inspired by SVD for invertible down/upscaling in latent space; designs adaptive detail prior (high-frequency dictionary) to compensate quantization loss; designs lightweight pixel semantic embedder for diffusion model conditioning.
en.result: Consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods in reconstruction fidelity and perceptual quality.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to combine reversible transformation and diffusion models to address information loss and semantic preservation in extreme image rescaling.

idea_spark.idea: Combining reversible transformation with diffusion models and using a high-frequency dictionary as prior provides an interpretable information compensation mechanism for extreme scaling.
idea_spark.risk: The high-frequency dictionary may overfit to training data distribution, limiting generalization to unseen structures; quantization errors in reversible transformation may still accumulate.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by singular value decomposition, decomposing images into reversible components and high-frequency details offers a new paradigm for extreme rescaling.

papers.title: To Call or Not to Call: A Framework to Assess and Optimize LLM Tool Calling
papers.summary: Agentic AI architectures augment LLMs with external tools, unlocking strong capabilities. However, tool use is not always beneficial; some calls may be redundant or even harmful. Effective tool use, therefore, hinges on a core LLM decision: whether to call or not call a tool, when performing a task. This decision is particularly challenging for web search tools, where the benefits of external information depend on the model's internal knowledge and its ability to integrate potentially noisy tool responses. We introduce a principled framework inspired by decision-making theory to evaluate web search tool-use decisions along three key factors: necessity, utility, and affordability. Our analysis combines two complementary lenses: a normative perspective that infers true need and utility from an optimal allocation of tool calls, and a descriptive perspective that infers the model's self-perceived need and utility from their observed behaviors. We find that models' perceived need and utility of tool calls are often misaligned with their true need and utility. Building on this framework, we train lightweight estimators of need and utility based on models' hidden states. Our estimators enable simple controllers that can improve decision quality and lead to stronger task performance than the self-perceived set up across three tasks and six models.

ai.tldr: 提出一个基于决策理论的框架，评估和优化大语言模型在任务中是否调用外部工具（特别是网络搜索工具）的决策。
ai.motivation: 工具调用并非总是有益，有时冗余甚至有害，核心在于模型能否正确判断何时需要调用工具。
ai.method: 从规范性（真实需求与效用）和描述性（模型自感知需求与效用）两个视角分析，并基于模型隐藏状态训练轻量级估计器来改进决策。
ai.result: 模型自感知的需求与效用常与真实情况错位，所提估计器能提升决策质量和任务性能。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解LLM工具调用决策的偏差，并提供一种轻量级优化方法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用模型内部状态训练轻量级控制器，替代或辅助模型自身的调用判断。
idea_spark.risk: 估计器依赖特定模型隐藏状态，跨模型泛化性可能有限；真实需求与效用的标注可能主观。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将决策理论中的规范-描述对比引入AI系统行为分析，启发更可靠的自主决策设计。

en.tldr: A decision-theoretic framework to assess and optimize whether LLMs should call external tools (especially web search) during tasks.
en.motivation: Tool calls are not always beneficial; the core challenge is the model's decision to call or not call a tool.
en.method: Analyzes from normative (true need/utility) and descriptive (self-perceived need/utility) perspectives, then trains lightweight estimators from hidden states to improve decisions.
en.result: Models' self-perceived need and utility often misalign with true values; the estimators improve decision quality and task performance.
en.help_to_user: Understands biases in LLM tool-calling decisions and offers a lightweight optimization approach.

idea_spark.idea: Use model internal states to train lightweight controllers that replace or assist the model's own calling judgment.
idea_spark.risk: Estimators depend on specific model hidden states, limiting cross-model generalization; labeling true need/utility can be subjective.
idea_spark.inspiration: Introducing normative-descriptive contrast from decision theory into AI behavior analysis inspires more reliable autonomous decision design.

papers.title: AEM: Adaptive Entropy Modulation for Multi-Turn Agentic Reinforcement Learning
papers.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has significantly advanced the ability of large language model (LLM) agents to interact with environments and solve multi-turn tasks. Yet effective training remains challenging, as sparse, outcome-only rewards make it difficult to assign credit to individual steps in an agent's action trajectory. A common remedy is to introduce dense intermediate supervision, such as process reward models or auxiliary self-supervised signals, but this increases supervision and tuning complexity and often generalizes poorly across tasks and domains. This paper presents AEM, a supervision-free credit assignment method that adaptively modulates entropy dynamics during RL training to achieve a more effective exploration-exploitation trade-off. Theoretically, we elevate entropy analysis from the token level to the response level to reduce token sampling variance and show that entropy drift under natural gradients is intrinsically governed by the product of the advantage and the relative response surprisal. Specifically, we derive a practical proxy to reshape training dynamics, enabling a natural transition from exploration to exploitation. Extensive experiments across various benchmarks and models ranging from 1.5B to 32B parameters demonstrate the effectiveness of AEM, including a notable 1.4 percent gain when integrated into a state-of-the-art baseline on the highly challenging SWE-bench-Verified benchmark.

ai.tldr: 提出一种免监督的信用分配方法AEM，通过自适应调节熵动态来优化多轮智能体强化学习的探索-利用平衡。
ai.motivation: 稀疏的最终奖励难以对智能体轨迹中的每一步进行信用分配，而引入密集中间监督会增加复杂度且泛化性差。
ai.method: 将熵分析从token级提升至response级，推导出自然梯度下熵漂移由优势与相对响应惊讶度的乘积决定，并设计实用代理重塑训练动态。
ai.result: 在1.5B至32B参数的多个模型和基准上验证有效，在SWE-bench-Verified上集成到SOTA基线后获得1.4%的提升。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需额外监督信号即可提升多轮任务中智能体训练效率的方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将response级熵分析与自然梯度结合，设计自适应探索-利用转换机制。
idea_spark.risk: 在极端稀疏奖励或长序列任务中，代理可能无法稳定收敛。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从信息论角度重新审视RL中的探索-利用平衡，启发更简洁的信用分配方案。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从信息论角度重新审视RL中的探索-利用平衡，启发更简洁的信用分配方案。

en.tldr: Proposes AEM, a supervision-free credit assignment method that adaptively modulates entropy dynamics to optimize exploration-exploitation trade-off in multi-turn agentic RL.
en.motivation: Sparse outcome-only rewards make credit assignment difficult; dense intermediate supervision increases complexity and generalizes poorly.
en.method: Elevates entropy analysis from token to response level, derives that entropy drift under natural gradients is governed by advantage times relative response surprisal, and designs a practical proxy to reshape training dynamics.
en.result: Validated across benchmarks and models from 1.5B to 32B parameters, achieving a 1.4% gain on SWE-bench-Verified when integrated into a SOTA baseline.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient way to improve agent training in multi-turn tasks without extra supervision signals.

idea_spark.idea: Combines response-level entropy analysis with natural gradients to design an adaptive exploration-exploitation transition mechanism.
idea_spark.risk: May struggle with convergence in extremely sparse reward or long-horizon tasks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Revisits exploration-exploitation from an information-theoretic perspective, inspiring simpler credit assignment solutions.

papers.title: InpaintSLat: Inpainting Structured 3D Latents via Initial Noise Optimization
papers.summary: We present a training-free approach for controllable 3D inpainting based on initial noise optimization. In the structured 3D latent diffusion framework, we observe that the underlying geometric structure is established during the early stages of the diffusion process and exhibits high sensitivity to the initial noise. Such characteristics compromise stability in tasks like inpainting and editing, where the model must ensure strict alignment with the existing context while synthesizing a new structure. In this paper, we introduce a strategy to optimize the initial noise within the structured 3D latent diffusion framework, ensuring high-fidelity 3D inpainting. Specifically, we update the initial noise by leveraging a backpropagation approximation grounded in the rectified flow model, with the spectral parameterization specially designed for robust and efficient structured 3D latent optimization. Experiments demonstrate consistent improvements in contextual consistency and prompt alignment over representative training-free inpainting baselines, establishing initial noise control as an independent dimension for 3D inpainting, orthogonal to conventional sampling trajectory manipulation.

ai.tldr: 提出一种无需训练的3D修复方法，通过优化初始噪声在结构化3D潜空间扩散框架中实现高保真修复。
ai.motivation: 现有3D修复方法在保持上下文一致性和结构稳定性方面存在不足，初始噪声对几何结构建立高度敏感，影响修复质量。
ai.method: 在结构化3D潜扩散框架中，利用基于整流流模型的反向传播近似优化初始噪声，并采用频谱参数化实现鲁棒高效优化。
ai.result: 在上下文一致性和提示对齐上持续优于代表性无训练修复基线，确立初始噪声控制为3D修复的独立维度。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需额外训练即可实现高质量3D修复的实用方法，适用于需要快速编辑或修复3D内容的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将初始噪声优化与条件控制结合，实现更精细的局部编辑或风格迁移。
idea_spark.risk: 优化过程可能增加计算开销，且对复杂场景的鲁棒性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受扩散模型早期阶段结构敏感性的启发，将噪声优化作为独立控制维度，启发其他生成任务中的潜在空间操控方法。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将初始噪声优化与条件控制结合，实现更精细的局部编辑或风格迁移。
idea_spark.risk: 优化过程可能增加计算开销，且对复杂场景的鲁棒性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受扩散模型早期阶段结构敏感性的启发，将噪声优化作为独立控制维度，启发其他生成任务中的潜在空间操控方法。

en.tldr: A training-free 3D inpainting method that optimizes initial noise in structured 3D latent diffusion for high-fidelity results.
en.motivation: Existing 3D inpainting struggles with contextual consistency and structural stability due to high sensitivity of initial noise on geometric structure formation.
en.method: Optimizes initial noise via backpropagation approximation based on rectified flow model, with spectral parameterization for robust and efficient structured 3D latent optimization.
en.result: Consistently outperforms representative training-free inpainting baselines in contextual consistency and prompt alignment, establishing initial noise control as an independent dimension for 3D inpainting.
en.help_to_user: Provides a practical training-free method for high-quality 3D inpainting, useful for quick editing or repairing 3D content.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining initial noise optimization with conditional control for finer local editing or style transfer.
idea_spark.risk: Optimization may increase computational cost, and robustness to complex scenes needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the structural sensitivity in early diffusion stages, treating noise optimization as an independent control dimension opens new avenues for latent space manipulation in other generative tasks.

papers.title: Essential Duality and Maximal Non-signalling Extensions in Algebraic Quantum Field Theory
papers.summary: We show that, under additivity, the maximal von Neumann algebra extension of $\mathcal{A}(O)$ inside $B(\mathcal{H})$ whose inner automorphisms are non-signalling with respect to all spacelike-separated regions is $\mathcal{A}(O')'$. Consequently, $\mathcal{A}(O)$ is maximal with respect to this property if and only if essential duality holds.   The proof is purely algebraic. When essential duality fails, we construct a proper extension all of whose inner automorphisms, and more generally all normal completely positive maps admitting Kraus operators in the algebra, are non-signalling. Under essential duality, any proper extension necessarily admits a signalling operation. An entropic formulation using Araki relative entropy provides a quantitative diagnostic of signalling, though it is not used in the proof.   Additional structural results include the wedge-intersection identity $\mathcal{A}(O')' = \bigcap_{W \supset O}\mathcal{A}(W)$ and equivalent characterisations of essential duality. These results identify essential duality as an operational maximality condition within the given representation.

ai.tldr: 在代数量子场论中，证明了在可加性条件下，最大非信号冯·诺依曼代数扩展等价于对偶区域的可换子代数，并指出本质对偶性等价于该最大性。
ai.motivation: 研究代数量子场论中区域代数的非信号扩展，并揭示本质对偶性的操作意义。
ai.method: 纯代数证明，利用冯·诺依曼代数扩展、内自同构和完全正映射的非信号性，辅以Araki相对熵的定量诊断。
ai.result: 本质对偶性成立时，任何真扩展必然包含信号操作；不成立时，存在非信号真扩展。还得到楔形交恒等式和本质对偶性的等价刻画。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解代数量子场论中区域代数的最大非信号扩展与本质对偶性的关系，以及操作意义上的最大性条件。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Araki相对熵作为信号性的定量诊断工具，可能用于检测量子场论中的信息泄露。
idea_spark.risk: 纯代数证明可能难以直接应用于具体物理模型，且相对熵的定量诊断需要进一步验证其可观测性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将代数场论中的对偶性概念与量子信息中的非信号原理结合，为量子引力中的信息悖论提供新视角。

zh.tldr: 在代数量子场论中，证明了在可加性条件下，最大非信号冯·诺依曼代数扩展等价于对偶区域的可换子代数，并指出本质对偶性等价于该最大性。
zh.motivation: 研究代数量子场论中区域代数的非信号扩展，并揭示本质对偶性的操作意义。
zh.method: 纯代数证明，利用冯·诺依曼代数扩展、内自同构和完全正映射的非信号性，辅以Araki相对熵的定量诊断。
zh.result: 本质对偶性成立时，任何真扩展必然包含信号操作；不成立时，存在非信号真扩展。还得到楔形交恒等式和本质对偶性的等价刻画。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解代数量子场论中区域代数的最大非信号扩展与本质对偶性的关系，以及操作意义上的最大性条件。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Araki相对熵作为信号性的定量诊断工具，可能用于检测量子场论中的信息泄露。
idea_spark.risk: 纯代数证明可能难以直接应用于具体物理模型，且相对熵的定量诊断需要进一步验证其可观测性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将代数场论中的对偶性概念与量子信息中的非信号原理结合，为量子引力中的信息悖论提供新视角。

en.tldr: In algebraic quantum field theory, under additivity, the maximal non-signalling von Neumann algebra extension of a local algebra is the commutant of the opposite region, and essential duality is equivalent to this maximality.
en.motivation: To characterize maximal non-signalling extensions of local algebras and reveal the operational meaning of essential duality.
en.method: Purely algebraic proof using von Neumann algebra extensions, inner automorphisms, and completely positive maps with Kraus operators, supplemented by Araki relative entropy for quantitative diagnostics.
en.result: Under essential duality, any proper extension admits a signalling operation; otherwise, a proper non-signalling extension exists. Wedge-intersection identity and equivalent characterizations of essential duality are also obtained.
en.help_to_user: Clarifies the relationship between maximal non-signalling extensions and essential duality in algebraic QFT, and provides an operational maximality condition.

idea_spark.idea: Use Araki relative entropy as a quantitative diagnostic of signalling, potentially to detect information leakage in quantum field theories.
idea_spark.risk: Purely algebraic proof may not directly apply to concrete physical models; the quantitative diagnostic needs further verification of observability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining duality in algebraic QFT with non-signalling principles from quantum information offers new perspectives on information paradoxes in quantum gravity.

papers.title: A Privacy-Preserving Approach to Conformance Checking
papers.summary: Conformance checking, one of the main process mining operations, aims to identify discrepancies between a process model and an event log. The model represents the expected behaviour, whereas the event log represents the actual process behaviour as captured in information systems records. Traditionally, the process model and the event log are both accessible to the business analyst performing the conformance checking. However, in some contexts, it is necessary to keep either the model or the log private to protect critical or sensitive information. In this paper, we propose a secure approach to conformance checking based on string processing algorithms and homomorphic encryption, where the process model and event log ar not visible to either the model's or event log's owner. The proposed technique is based on alignments, a well-known formalism used for conformance checking. An evaluation is performed using a synthetic and a real-world event log, showing that conformance checking can be securely computed at the expense of high memory and processing requirements.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于同态加密和字符串处理算法的隐私保护一致性检查方法，使流程模型和事件日志在双方不可见的情况下完成对齐计算。
ai.motivation: 传统一致性检查需要分析者同时访问流程模型和事件日志，但在某些场景下模型或日志包含敏感信息，需要保护隐私。
ai.method: 利用同态加密对字符串处理算法进行加密计算，实现基于对齐的一致性检查，模型和日志的所有者均无法看到对方的数据。
ai.result: 在合成和真实事件日志上的实验表明，该方法能够安全地完成一致性检查，但需要较高的内存和处理开销。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户在保护敏感流程模型或事件日志隐私的前提下，仍能进行一致性检查，发现实际行为与预期行为的偏差。

idea_spark.idea: 将同态加密与更高效的字符串匹配算法（如KMP或Boyer-Moore）结合，降低计算开销。
idea_spark.risk: 同态加密的计算开销较大，可能限制在大规模日志上的实际应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴安全多方计算中更高效的协议（如混淆电路）来优化对齐计算，减少加密操作次数。

idea_spark.idea: 将同态加密与更高效的字符串匹配算法（如KMP或Boyer-Moore）结合，降低计算开销。
idea_spark.risk: 同态加密的计算开销较大，可能限制在大规模日志上的实际应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴安全多方计算中更高效的协议（如混淆电路）来优化对齐计算，减少加密操作次数。

en.tldr: Proposes a privacy-preserving conformance checking approach using homomorphic encryption and string processing algorithms, enabling alignment computation without revealing the process model or event log to either party.
en.motivation: Traditional conformance checking requires the analyst to access both the process model and event log, which may contain sensitive information that needs protection.
en.method: Leverages homomorphic encryption to perform string processing algorithms for alignment-based conformance checking, ensuring neither the model owner nor the log owner sees the other's data.
en.result: Experiments on synthetic and real-world event logs show that conformance checking can be securely computed, albeit with high memory and processing requirements.
en.help_to_user: Enables users to perform conformance checking while preserving the privacy of sensitive process models or event logs, identifying deviations between expected and actual behavior.

idea_spark.idea: Combine homomorphic encryption with more efficient string matching algorithms (e.g., KMP or Boyer-Moore) to reduce computational overhead.
idea_spark.risk: The high computational cost of homomorphic encryption may limit practical application on large-scale logs.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw inspiration from more efficient secure multi-party computation protocols (e.g., garbled circuits) to optimize alignment computation and reduce encryption operations.

papers.title: Empowering Heterogeneous Graph Foundation Models via Decoupled Relation Alignment
papers.summary: While Graph Foundation Models (GFMs) have achieved remarkable success in homogeneous graphs, extending them to multi-domain heterogeneous graphs (MDHGs) remains a formidable challenge due to cross-type feature shifts and intra-domain relation gaps. Existing global feature alignment methods (PCA or SVD) enforce a shared feature space blindly, which distorts type-specific semantics and disrupts original topologies, inevitably leading to "Type Collapse" and "Relation Confusion". To address these fundamental limitations, we propose Decoupled relation Subspace Alignment (DRSA), a novel, plug-and-play relation-driven alignment framework. DRSA fundamentally shifts the paradigm by decoupling feature semantics from relation structures. Specifically, it introduces a dual-relation subspace projection mechanism to coordinate cross-type interactions within a shared low-rank relation subspace explicitly. Furthermore, a feature-structure decoupled representation is designed to decompose aligned features into a semantic projection component and a structural residual term, adaptively absorbing intra-domain variations. Optimized via a stable alternating minimization strategy based on Block Coordinate Descent, DRSA constructs a well-calibrated, structure-aware latent space. Extensive experiments on multiple real-world benchmark datasets demonstrate that DRSA can be seamlessly integrated as a universal preprocessing module, significantly and consistently enhancing the cross-domain and few-shot knowledge transfer capabilities of state-of-the-art GFMs. The code is available at: https://github.com/zhengziyu77/DSRA.

ai.tldr: 提出解耦关系子空间对齐（DRSA）框架，解决异构图基础模型在多域异构图中因特征偏移和关系间隙导致的类型坍塌与关系混淆问题。
ai.motivation: 现有全局特征对齐方法（如PCA、SVD）强制共享特征空间，扭曲类型特定语义并破坏原始拓扑，导致类型坍塌和关系混淆。
ai.method: DRSA通过双关系子空间投影机制显式协调跨类型交互，并设计特征-结构解耦表示，将对齐特征分解为语义投影分量和结构残差项，采用块坐标下降的交替最小化策略优化。
ai.result: 在多个真实基准数据集上，DRSA作为通用预处理模块显著且一致地提升了最先进图基础模型的跨域和少样本知识迁移能力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过解耦特征与关系结构来提升异构图基础模型在多领域场景下的泛化性能。

idea_spark.idea: 将特征-结构解耦表示与交替优化策略结合，为多模态或跨任务对齐提供新范式。
idea_spark.risk: 解耦可能引入额外计算开销，且对关系子空间维度的选择敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受块坐标下降启发，可探索更高效的优化策略（如随机坐标下降）以加速收敛。

en.tldr: Proposes Decoupled Relation Subspace Alignment (DRSA) to address type collapse and relation confusion in heterogeneous graph foundation models for multi-domain heterogeneous graphs.
en.motivation: Existing global feature alignment methods (e.g., PCA, SVD) enforce a shared feature space blindly, distorting type-specific semantics and disrupting original topologies, leading to type collapse and relation confusion.
en.method: DRSA introduces a dual-relation subspace projection mechanism to coordinate cross-type interactions and a feature-structure decoupled representation that decomposes aligned features into a semantic projection component and a structural residual term, optimized via alternating minimization based on Block Coordinate Descent.
en.result: Extensive experiments on multiple real-world benchmarks show DRSA as a universal preprocessing module significantly and consistently enhances cross-domain and few-shot knowledge transfer of state-of-the-art GFMs.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how decoupling feature semantics from relation structures improves generalization of heterogeneous graph foundation models across domains.

idea_spark.idea: Combining feature-structure decoupled representation with alternating optimization offers a new paradigm for multi-modal or cross-task alignment.
idea_spark.risk: Decoupling may introduce extra computational cost and sensitivity to the choice of relation subspace dimension.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by Block Coordinate Descent, exploring more efficient optimization strategies (e.g., stochastic coordinate descent) could accelerate convergence.

papers.title: Learning How and What to Memorize: Cognition-Inspired Two-Stage Optimization for Evolving Memory
papers.summary: Large language model (LLM) agents require long-term user memory for consistent personalization, but limited context windows hinder tracking evolving preferences over long interactions. Existing memory systems mainly rely on static, hand-crafted update rules; although reinforcement learning (RL)-based agents learn memory updates, sparse outcome rewards provide weak supervision, resulting in unstable long-horizon optimization. Drawing on memory schema theory and the functional division between prefrontal regions and hippocampus regions, we introduce MemCoE, a cognition-inspired two-stage optimization framework that learns how memory should be organized and what information to update. In the first stage, we propose Memory Guideline Induction to optimize a global guideline via contrastive feedback interpreted as textual gradients; in the second stage, Guideline-Aligned Memory Policy Optimization uses the induced guideline to define structured process rewards and performs multi-turn RL to learn a guideline-following memory evolution policy. We evaluate on three personalization memory benchmarks, covering explicit/implicit preference and different sizes and noise, and observe consistent improvements over strong baselines with favorable robustness, transferability, and efficiency.

ai.tldr: 提出MemCoE框架，受认知科学启发，通过两阶段优化学习记忆的组织方式和更新内容，提升大语言模型代理的长期个性化记忆能力。
ai.motivation: 现有记忆系统依赖静态手工规则，强化学习方法因稀疏奖励导致长程优化不稳定，无法有效跟踪用户随时间变化的偏好。
ai.method: 第一阶段通过对比反馈生成文本梯度，归纳全局记忆准则；第二阶段利用该准则定义结构化过程奖励，进行多轮强化学习训练记忆演化策略。
ai.result: 在三个个性化记忆基准上，涵盖显式/隐式偏好、不同规模和噪声，一致优于强基线，并展现出良好的鲁棒性、可迁移性和效率。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何让AI代理在长期交互中记住并适应用户不断变化的偏好，提升个性化体验。

idea_spark.idea: 将认知科学中的记忆图式理论与文本梯度结合，为强化学习提供结构化过程奖励，解决稀疏奖励问题。
idea_spark.risk: 准则归纳可能引入偏差，多轮强化学习训练成本较高，需验证在更复杂任务上的泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受大脑前额叶与海马体功能分工启发，将记忆的组织（如何记忆）与内容（记忆什么）分离优化。

en.tldr: Proposes MemCoE, a cognition-inspired two-stage optimization framework that learns how to organize memory and what to update, enhancing long-term user memory for LLM agents.
en.motivation: Existing memory systems rely on static hand-crafted rules, and RL-based methods suffer from sparse outcome rewards leading to unstable long-horizon optimization, failing to track evolving user preferences.
en.method: Stage 1 induces a global memory guideline via contrastive feedback interpreted as textual gradients; Stage 2 uses the guideline to define structured process rewards and performs multi-turn RL to learn a guideline-following memory evolution policy.
en.result: Consistent improvements over strong baselines on three personalization memory benchmarks covering explicit/implicit preferences, different sizes, and noise, with favorable robustness, transferability, and efficiency.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how AI agents can remember and adapt to changing user preferences over long interactions, improving personalization.

idea_spark.idea: Combining memory schema theory from cognitive science with textual gradients to provide structured process rewards for RL, addressing sparse reward problems.
idea_spark.risk: Guideline induction may introduce bias; multi-turn RL training can be costly; generalization to more complex tasks needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the functional division between prefrontal regions (how to memorize) and hippocampus (what to memorize), separating memory organization from content update.

papers.title: Time-Interval-Aware Disentangled Expert Modeling for Next-Basket Recommendation
papers.summary: Next-basket recommendation (NBR) is a type of recommendation that aims to predict a set of items a user will purchase based on their historical transaction basket sequences. It is governed by a dynamic interplay between two distinct user intents: habitual repurchase, which involves repeating past behaviors, and exploratory interest, which involves discovering new items. However, existing NBR methods generally suffer from two limitations: (1) they often entangle these conflicting motives within a single representation, causing habits to overshadow discovery, and (2) they rely on discrete sequential modeling that ignores continuous-time intervals and item-specific periodicities. In this paper, we propose a novel solution named Time-Interval Disentangled Experts (TIDE) to address these challenges. TIDE incorporates a Hawkes-enhanced Fourier Time Encoding to capture item-specific temporal periodicities and dynamic decay. To decouple user intentions, TIDE utilizes a dual-expert architecture that integrates a Habit Expert for recurring needs and a Pattern-Guided Exploration Expert for discovery. Combined with an item-aware gating mechanism, TIDE adaptively balances repurchase and exploration. Extensive experiments on four diverse real-world datasets demonstrate that TIDE consistently outperforms representative state-of-the-art NBR methods.

ai.tldr: 提出时间间隔感知的解耦专家模型（TIDE），用于下一购物篮推荐，通过分离习惯性复购和探索性兴趣，并利用Hawkes增强傅里叶时间编码捕捉物品级时间周期性，在四个数据集上超越现有方法。
ai.motivation: 现有方法将冲突的用户意图（习惯复购与探索兴趣）纠缠在单一表示中，且忽略连续时间间隔和物品特定周期性，导致习惯掩盖探索。
ai.method: 采用Hawkes增强傅里叶时间编码建模时间周期性与动态衰减；设计双专家架构（习惯专家与模式引导探索专家）解耦意图；结合物品感知门控机制自适应平衡复购与探索。
ai.result: 在四个真实数据集上，TIDE一致优于代表性最先进方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助推荐系统更精准地预测用户下一购物篮，同时兼顾重复购买和探索新物品的需求。

idea_spark.idea: 将Hawkes过程与傅里叶编码结合，用于捕捉物品级时间周期性，可启发其他时间序列建模场景。
idea_spark.risk: 双专家架构可能增加模型复杂度，需注意过拟合和计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从用户行为中分离习惯与探索的机制，可启发其他涉及矛盾意图的推荐或决策系统设计。

en.tldr: Proposes Time-Interval Disentangled Experts (TIDE) for next-basket recommendation, decoupling habitual repurchase and exploratory interest via a dual-expert architecture and Hawkes-enhanced Fourier time encoding, outperforming state-of-the-art on four datasets.
en.motivation: Existing methods entangle conflicting user intents (habit vs. exploration) in a single representation and ignore continuous time intervals and item-specific periodicities, causing habits to overshadow discovery.
en.method: Uses Hawkes-enhanced Fourier time encoding to capture item-specific temporal periodicities and decay; employs a dual-expert architecture (Habit Expert and Pattern-Guided Exploration Expert) to decouple intents; combines with an item-aware gating mechanism to adaptively balance repurchase and exploration.
en.result: TIDE consistently outperforms representative state-of-the-art NBR methods on four diverse real-world datasets.
en.help_to_user: Helps recommendation systems more accurately predict a user's next basket by balancing habitual repurchase and exploratory discovery.

idea_spark.idea: Combining Hawkes processes with Fourier encoding for item-specific temporal periodicity could inspire other time series modeling scenarios.
idea_spark.risk: The dual-expert architecture may increase model complexity, requiring attention to overfitting and computational cost.
idea_spark.inspiration: The mechanism of separating habit and exploration from user behavior can inspire the design of other recommendation or decision systems involving conflicting intents.

papers.title: How Frontier LLMs Adapt to Neurodivergence Context: A Measurement Framework for Surface vs. Structural Change in System-Prompted Responses
papers.summary: We examine if frontier chat-based large language models (LLMs) adjust their outputs based on neurodivergence (ND) context in system prompts and describe the nature of these adjustments. Specifically, we propose NDBench, a 576-output benchmark involving two frontier models, three system prompt types (baseline, ND-profile assertion, and ND-profile assertion with explicit instructions for adjustments), four canonical ND profiles, and 24 prompts across four categories, one of which involves an adversarial masking strategy.   Four trends emerge consistently from our findings. First, LLMs show significant adaptation under ND context, where fully instructed conditions yield lengthier and more structured outputs, characterized by higher token counts, more headings, and more granular steps (p < 10^-8, Holm-corrected). Second, such adaptation is largely structural in nature: although list density does not change much, there is a marked rise in the frequency of headings and per-step detail. Third, ND persona assertion alone fails to suppress potentially harmful tendencies, as masking-reinforcement decreases only in explicitly instructed cases (36-44% reduction); the reduction rate barely changes in persona assertion conditions.   Moreover, reliability analysis of LLM-based harm assessment reveals that only two out of the six dimensions (masking and reinforcement, validation quality) exceed the pre-defined inter-judge agreement criterion (alpha >= 0.67) and thus can be considered primary results.   NDBench is made publicly available along with its prompts, outputs, code, and other resources, forming a reproducible framework for auditing future LLMs' adaptation to ND awareness.

ai.tldr: 提出NDBench基准，测量前沿LLM在系统提示中根据神经多样性上下文调整输出的表面与结构变化。
ai.motivation: 现有研究缺乏系统评估LLM对神经多样性上下文适应性的方法，尤其是区分表面调整与结构变化。
ai.method: 构建576输出基准，涉及两个前沿模型、三种系统提示类型、四种神经多样性画像及24个提示（含对抗掩蔽策略），通过统计检验和可靠性分析评估调整。
ai.result: LLM在明确指令下产生更长、更结构化的输出（标题和步骤细节增加），但仅明确指令减少有害倾向（掩蔽强化降低36-44%），且仅两个危害评估维度达到可靠性标准。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解LLM在神经多样性上下文中的实际适应程度，区分表面与结构变化，并识别可靠评估维度。

idea_spark.idea: 开发动态系统提示模板，根据用户神经多样性特征自动调整指令强度，平衡结构优化与有害倾向抑制。
idea_spark.risk: 过度依赖结构变化可能忽略语义层面的细微适应，且可靠性低的评估维度可能误导结论。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴对抗掩蔽策略，设计更精细的提示扰动来探测LLM的隐性偏见与适应边界。

en.tldr: Proposes NDBench, a benchmark measuring surface vs. structural changes in frontier LLMs' responses when system prompts include neurodivergence context.
en.motivation: Lack of systematic methods to evaluate LLM adaptation to neurodivergence context, especially distinguishing surface adjustments from structural changes.
en.method: Constructs a 576-output benchmark with two frontier models, three system prompt types, four ND profiles, and 24 prompts (including adversarial masking), using statistical tests and reliability analysis.

en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the actual extent of LLM adaptation to neurodivergence context, distinguish surface vs. structural changes, and identify reliable evaluation dimensions.

idea_spark.idea: Develop dynamic system prompt templates that automatically adjust instruction intensity based on user neurodivergence profiles, balancing structural optimization and harm reduction.
idea_spark.risk: Over-reliance on structural changes may overlook subtle semantic adaptations, and low-reliability evaluation dimensions could mislead conclusions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage adversarial masking strategies to design more refined prompt perturbations that probe LLMs' implicit biases and adaptation boundaries.

papers.title: Unsupervised Denoising of Real Clinical Low Dose Liver CT with Perceptual Attention Networks
papers.summary: With the development of deep learning, medical image processing has been widely used to assist clinical research. This paper focuses on the denoising problem of low-dose computed tomography using deep learning. Although low-dose computed tomography reduces radiation exposure to patients, it also introduces more noise, which may interfere with visual interpretation by physicians and affect diagnostic results. To address this problem, inspired by Cycle-GAN for unsupervised learning, this paper proposes an end-to-end unsupervised low-dose computed tomography denoising framework. The proposed framework combines a U-Net structure for multi-scale feature extraction, an attention mechanism for feature fusion, and a residual network for feature transformation. It also introduces perceptual loss to improve the network for the characteristics of medical images. In addition, we construct a real low-dose computed tomography dataset and design a large number of comparative experiments to validate the proposed method, using both image-based evaluation metrics and medical evaluation criteria. Compared with classical methods, the main advantage of this paper is that it addresses the limitation that real clinical data cannot be directly used for supervised learning, while still achieving excellent performance. The experimental results are also professionally evaluated by imaging physicians and meet clinical needs.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于Cycle-GAN的无监督低剂量CT去噪框架，结合U-Net、注意力机制和感知损失，在真实临床数据集上取得优异效果。
ai.motivation: 低剂量CT虽减少辐射但引入噪声，影响诊断；真实临床数据无法直接用于监督学习。
ai.method: 端到端无监督框架，包含U-Net多尺度特征提取、注意力机制特征融合、残差网络特征变换，并引入感知损失。
ai.result: 在真实低剂量CT数据集上，图像指标和医学评估均优于经典方法，满足临床需求。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何用无监督深度学习去除低剂量CT噪声，无需配对数据。

idea_spark.idea: 将感知损失与注意力机制结合，提升无监督去噪对医学图像细节的保留能力。
idea_spark.risk: 无监督方法可能在某些噪声模式上效果不稳定，需更多临床验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用Cycle-GAN的无监督思路，结合医学图像特有的感知损失，可启发其他无监督医学图像增强任务。

en.tldr: Proposes an unsupervised low-dose CT denoising framework based on Cycle-GAN, combining U-Net, attention mechanism, and perceptual loss, achieving excellent results on a real clinical dataset.
en.motivation: Low-dose CT reduces radiation but introduces noise affecting diagnosis; real clinical data cannot be directly used for supervised learning.
en.method: End-to-end unsupervised framework with U-Net for multi-scale feature extraction, attention mechanism for feature fusion, residual network for feature transformation, and perceptual loss.
en.result: Outperforms classical methods in both image metrics and medical evaluation on a real low-dose CT dataset, meeting clinical needs.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to denoise low-dose CT using unsupervised deep learning without paired data.

idea_spark.idea: Combining perceptual loss with attention mechanism enhances detail preservation in unsupervised denoising.
idea_spark.risk: Unsupervised methods may be unstable for certain noise patterns and require more clinical validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: The Cycle-GAN unsupervised approach with medical-specific perceptual loss can inspire other unsupervised medical image enhancement tasks.

papers.title: Delay-Doppler Domain Channel Estimation: What if Sparsity is Unknown?
papers.summary: Sparsity in the delay-Doppler (DD) domain enables efficient channel estimation, but the realization-wise sparsity level is rarely known in advance, and it fluctuates. What if we could estimate the channel without ever knowing how many delays or Dopplers are active? This paper answers that question. We propose a sparsity-agnostic structured estimator that requires no prior knowledge of delay or Doppler sparsity budgets. The key idea is to exploit the Cartesian-product structure of DD support (active delays share a common Doppler set) and to select the support dimensions directly from the data via the Bayesian information criterion. We instantiate the framework on an affine frequency division multiplexing system, where the observation model naturally admits an on-grid DD representation. Numerical results demonstrate that it recovers the exact support with high probability and achieves near-oracle channel reconstruction accuracy, consistently outperforming fixed-budget baselines and sparse Bayesian learning. The approach is waveform-agnostic and offers a practical, adaptive solution for DD-domain channel estimation under unknown and time-varying sparsity.

ai.tldr: 提出一种无需先验稀疏度信息的延迟-多普勒域信道估计方法，利用笛卡尔积结构和贝叶斯信息准则自适应选择支撑集，在仿射频分复用系统中实现近最优重构。
ai.motivation: 延迟-多普勒域信道估计依赖稀疏性，但实际稀疏度未知且时变，现有固定预算方法性能受限。
ai.method: 利用DD支撑的笛卡尔积结构（活跃延迟共享共同多普勒集），通过贝叶斯信息准则从数据中直接选择支撑维度，构建稀疏无关的结构化估计器。
ai.result: 高概率恢复精确支撑，达到近最优信道重构精度，一致优于固定预算基线和稀疏贝叶斯学习。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种实用、自适应的DD域信道估计方案，适用于稀疏度未知且时变的实际通信场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将贝叶斯信息准则与结构化稀疏模型结合，实现无需超参数调优的在线支撑选择。
idea_spark.risk: 对支撑结构的假设（如共同多普勒集）在非理想信道中可能不成立，需验证鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用数据驱动的模型选择替代先验稀疏度假设，启发其他领域（如雷达、图像处理）的自适应稀疏估计设计。

idea_spark.inspiration: 利用数据驱动的模型选择替代先验稀疏度假设，启发其他领域（如雷达、图像处理）的自适应稀疏估计设计。

en.tldr: Proposes a sparsity-agnostic structured estimator for delay-Doppler domain channel estimation, leveraging Cartesian-product structure and Bayesian information criterion to select support dimensions adaptively, achieving near-oracle reconstruction in affine frequency division multiplexing systems.
en.motivation: Sparsity in DD domain enables efficient channel estimation, but the actual sparsity level is unknown and time-varying, limiting fixed-budget methods.
en.method: Exploits the Cartesian-product structure of DD support (active delays share a common Doppler set) and selects support dimensions directly from data via Bayesian information criterion, forming a sparsity-agnostic structured estimator.
en.result: Recovers exact support with high probability, achieves near-oracle channel reconstruction accuracy, and consistently outperforms fixed-budget baselines and sparse Bayesian learning.
en.help_to_user: Provides a practical, adaptive solution for DD-domain channel estimation under unknown and time-varying sparsity.

idea_spark.idea: Combine Bayesian information criterion with structured sparsity models for online support selection without hyperparameter tuning.
idea_spark.risk: The assumption of common Doppler set may fail in non-ideal channels; robustness needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using data-driven model selection to replace prior sparsity assumptions inspires adaptive sparse estimation in other fields like radar and image processing.

papers.title: IdentiFace: Multi-Modal Iterative Diffusion Framework for Identifiable Suspect Face Generation in Crime Investigations
papers.summary: Suspect face generation remains a technical challenge in crime investigations. Traditional sketch-drawing workflows suffer from low efficiency and quality, while diffusion-based approaches still face intrinsic limitations on conditional ambiguity for text-to-image models and sampling variance for one-shot generation. We proposed IdentiFace, a novel diffusion-based framework for identifiable suspect face generation, which addressed these issues through (1) multi-modal input design to strengthen conditional control, and (2) an iterative generation pipeline enabling identifiable feature adjustment. We additionally contributed a facial identity loss and two task-specific datasets. Comprehensive experiments on synthetic datasets and in real-world scenarios indicate that IdentiFace achieves superior performance over existing methods, especially in terms of identity retrieval, and shows strong potential for practical applications.

ai.tldr: 提出IdentiFace，一种基于扩散模型的多模态迭代框架，用于生成可识别的嫌疑人面部图像，在身份检索上优于现有方法。
ai.motivation: 传统素描方法效率低、质量差，现有扩散模型存在条件模糊和单次生成方差大的问题，难以生成可识别的嫌疑人面部。
ai.method: 采用多模态输入（文本+其他模态）增强条件控制，并设计迭代生成管线以逐步调整可识别特征，同时引入面部身份损失函数。
ai.result: 在合成数据集和真实场景中，IdentiFace在身份检索等指标上显著优于现有方法，展现出实际应用潜力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助刑侦人员快速生成高辨识度的嫌疑人面部图像，提升破案效率。

idea_spark.idea: 结合迭代反馈机制与多模态条件，可进一步探索交互式嫌疑人画像系统，允许目击者逐步修正生成结果。
idea_spark.risk: 生成的面部可能被误用或引发隐私争议，需严格限定于执法场景并配合伦理审查。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将身份损失与扩散模型结合，启发其他需要细粒度身份控制的生成任务，如医学影像中的个体化合成。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助刑侦人员快速生成高辨识度的嫌疑人面部图像，提升破案效率。

idea_spark.risk: 生成的面部可能被误用或引发隐私争议，需严格限定于执法场景并配合伦理审查。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将身份损失与扩散模型结合，启发其他需要细粒度身份控制的生成任务，如医学影像中的个体化合成。

en.tldr: Proposes IdentiFace, a multi-modal iterative diffusion framework for generating identifiable suspect faces, outperforming existing methods in identity retrieval.
en.motivation: Traditional sketch workflows are inefficient and low-quality; existing diffusion models suffer from conditional ambiguity and one-shot sampling variance, hindering identifiable suspect face generation.
en.method: Employs multi-modal input to strengthen conditional control, an iterative generation pipeline for adjustable identifiable features, and a facial identity loss.
en.result: On synthetic and real-world datasets, IdentiFace achieves superior performance, especially in identity retrieval, showing strong practical potential.
en.help_to_user: Assists forensic investigators in rapidly generating high-identifiability suspect faces, improving crime-solving efficiency.

idea_spark.idea: Combining iterative feedback with multi-modal conditioning could lead to an interactive suspect sketching system, allowing witnesses to refine results progressively.
idea_spark.risk: Generated faces may be misused or raise privacy concerns; strict deployment in law enforcement with ethical review is necessary.
idea_spark.inspiration: Integrating identity loss with diffusion models inspires other fine-grained identity-controlled generation tasks, such as personalized synthesis in medical imaging.

papers.title: Affordance Agent Harness: Verification-Gated Skill Orchestration
papers.summary: Affordance grounding requires identifying where and how an agent should interact in open-world scenes, where actionable regions are often small, occluded, reflective, and visually ambiguous. Recent systems therefore combine multiple skills (e.g., detection, segmentation, interaction-imagination), yet most orchestrate them with fixed pipelines that are poorly matched to per-instance difficulty, offer limited targeted recovery from intermediate errors, and fail to reuse experience from recurring objects. These failures expose a systems problem: test-time grounding must acquire the right evidence, decide whether that evidence is reliable enough to commit, and do so under bounded inference cost without access to labels. We propose Affordance Agent Harness, a closed-loop runtime that unifies heterogeneous skills with an evidence store and cost control, retrieves episodic memories to provide priors for recurring categories, and employs a Router to adaptively select and parameterize skills. An affordance-specific Verifier then gates commitments using self-consistency, cross-scale stability, and evidence sufficiency, triggering targeted retries before a final judge fuses accumulated evidence and trajectories into the prediction. Experiments on multiple affordance benchmarks and difficulty-controlled subsets show a stronger accuracy-cost Pareto frontier than fixed-pipeline baselines, improving grounding quality while reducing average skill calls and latency. Project page: https://tenplusgood.github.io/a-harness-page/.

ai.tldr: 提出一种闭环运行时系统，通过验证门控和技能编排，在开放世界场景中高效、可靠地完成可供性定位。
ai.motivation: 现有固定流水线方法难以适应实例难度变化，中间错误无法针对性恢复，且不能复用经验。
ai.method: 统一异构技能与证据存储和成本控制，检索情景记忆提供先验，路由器自适应选择技能，验证器基于自一致性、跨尺度稳定性和证据充分性门控提交，最终融合预测。
ai.result: 在多个可供性基准和难度控制子集上，相比固定流水线基线，在提升定位质量的同时减少了技能调用次数和延迟。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过验证门控和记忆检索来提升开放世界可供性定位的鲁棒性和效率。

idea_spark.idea: 将验证器设计为可学习的，并引入主动学习策略以自动收集难例。
idea_spark.risk: 验证器可能引入额外计算开销，且记忆检索在未见类别上可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类在不确定时反复检查并调用经验的启发，设计闭环验证机制。

idea_spark.idea: 将验证器设计为可学习的，并引入主动学习策略以自动收集难例。
idea_spark.risk: 验证器可能引入额外计算开销，且记忆检索在未见类别上可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类在不确定时反复检查并调用经验的启发，设计闭环验证机制。

en.tldr: Proposes a closed-loop runtime that uses verification gating and skill orchestration for efficient and reliable affordance grounding in open-world scenes.
en.motivation: Existing fixed pipelines poorly adapt to per-instance difficulty, lack targeted recovery from intermediate errors, and fail to reuse experience.
en.method: Unifies heterogeneous skills with an evidence store and cost control, retrieves episodic memories for priors, adaptively selects skills via a Router, gates commitments with a Verifier using self-consistency, cross-scale stability, and evidence sufficiency, and fuses evidence into final prediction.
en.result: On multiple affordance benchmarks and difficulty-controlled subsets, achieves a stronger accuracy-cost Pareto frontier than fixed-pipeline baselines, improving grounding quality while reducing skill calls and latency.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how verification gating and memory retrieval improve robustness and efficiency in open-world affordance grounding.

idea_spark.idea: Design a learnable verifier with active learning to automatically collect hard examples.
idea_spark.risk: The verifier may introduce extra computation, and memory retrieval may fail on unseen categories.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by humans re-checking and recalling experience under uncertainty, leading to a closed-loop verification mechanism.

papers.title: Pose-Aware Diffusion for 3D Generation
papers.summary: Generating pose-aligned 3D objects is challenging due to the spatial mismatches and transformation ambiguities inherent in decoupled canonical-then-rotate paradigms. To this end, we introduce Pose-Aware Diffusion (PAD), a novel end-to-end diffusion framework that synthesizes 3D geometry directly within the observation space. By unprojecting monocular depth into a partial point cloud and explicitly injecting it as a 3D geometric anchor, PAD abandons canonical assumptions to enforce rigorous spatial supervision. This native generation intrinsically resolves pose ambiguity, producing high-fidelity pose-aligned assets. Extensive experiments demonstrate that PAD achieves superior geometric alignment and image-to-3D correspondence compared to state-of-the-art methods. Additionally, PAD naturally extends to compositional 3D scene reconstruction via a simple union of independently generated objects, highlighting its robust ability to preserve precise spatial layouts.

ai.tldr: 提出PAD，一种端到端扩散框架，直接在观测空间生成3D几何，通过单目深度反投影为点云锚点，消除姿态歧义，实现高保真姿态对齐。
ai.motivation: 解耦的规范-旋转范式存在空间错位和变换歧义，难以生成姿态对齐的3D物体。
ai.method: 将单目深度反投影为部分点云作为3D几何锚点，注入扩散模型，在观测空间直接生成3D几何，放弃规范假设。
ai.result: 在几何对齐和图像到3D对应上优于现有方法，并自然扩展到组合场景重建。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需规范空间假设、直接生成姿态对齐3D物体的新方法，简化流程并提升对齐精度。

idea_spark.idea: 结合多视角深度信息或时序深度，进一步提升复杂场景下的姿态对齐鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖单目深度估计质量，深度误差可能影响生成几何的准确性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 放弃规范空间、直接在观测空间生成的思想，可启发其他领域（如人体姿态生成）的端到端方法设计。
